scope_note: QA Degree-4 Cyclotomic Orbit over Z[zeta_5] cert [396]. Primary sources: Washington (1997) 'Introduction to Cyclotomic Fields' ISBN 978-0-387-94762-4 (companion matrix for Phi_5(x), Z[zeta_n] module structure, Galois action, partial trace formula); Ireland & Rosen (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.14 (cyclotomic integers, norm forms, factorization of primes p ≡ 1 mod n); Lang (1994) 'Algebraic Number Theory' ISBN 978-1-4612-0853-2 §I.5 (companion matrix as Z-module representation, relation between char poly and minimal poly); Neukirch (1999) 'Algebraic Number Theory' ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8 §II.10 (Hecke characters, CM fields, L-function factorization). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): The companion matrix C of Phi_5(x) = x^4+x^3+x^2+x+1 satisfies C^5 = I_4 (exact integer arithmetic), the Galois matrix G (sigma: zeta_5->zeta_5^2) satisfies G^4 = I_4, the quartic norm form N(a+b*zeta_5) = a^4-a^3b+a^2b^2-ab^3+b^4 recovers prime norms for generators at p in {11,31,61,181}, prime generators have 5-periodic C-orbits, and the partial trace Tr_{Q(zeta_5)/Q(sqrt(5))}(a+b*zeta_5) = (2a-b)+b*phi takes values in Z[phi]. Checks: (C1) C^5 = I_4 (5-periodicity, exact order 5); (C2) G^4 = I_4 (Galois order 4); (C3) norm form verified for 8 cases at 4 distinct primes; (C4) C-orbits of prime generators have period exactly 5; (C5) partial trace formula verified for 4 prime generators, values in Z[phi]. Theorem NT compliance: all arithmetic is exact integer matrix operations and polynomial evaluation; no floats in the QA layer; the relation 'zeta_5+zeta_5^4 = (sqrt(5)-1)/2' is referenced in the docstring as a motivating fact, never used as a float computation. Extends cert [391] (Cassini identity, sigma=phi-multiplication on Z[phi]); cert [394] (GL_1 Frobenius from degree-2 orbit iteration); cert [395] (GL_2 Weil bound, discriminant sign flip). Infrastructure for cert [397] FUTURE (CM Hecke character: a_f(p) from Z[zeta_5] orbit, needs CM form LMFDB data). Langlands chain position: this cert establishes the degree-4 arithmetic layer between the degree-2 QA orbit arithmetic (certs [391-395]) and the full CM L-function factorization. The 5-periodicity C^5=I is the degree-4 analog of det(M^p)=(-1)^p (Cassini, cert [391]). Derived 2026-06-12.
